metadata.name: mpl_account_compression
metadata.description: Metaplex Fork of Solana Program Library Account Compression Program

instructions.name: append
docs: This instruction allows the tree's `authority` to append a new leaf to the tree
without having to supply a proof.
Learn more about SPL
ConcurrentMerkleTree
[here](https://github.com/solana-labs/solana-program-library/tree/master/libraries/concurrent-merkle-tree)

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that controls write-access to the tree
Typically a program, e.g., the Bubblegum contract validates that leaves are valid NFTs.

accounts.name: noop
docs: Program used to emit changelogs as cpi instruction data.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that controls write-access to the tree

instructions.name: init_empty_merkle_tree
docs: Creates a new merkle tree with maximum leaf capacity of `power(2, max_depth)`
and a minimum concurrency limit of `max_buffer_size`.
Concurrency limit represents the # of replace instructions that can be successfully
executed with proofs dated for the same root. For example, a maximum buffer size of 1024
means that a minimum of 1024 replaces can be executed before a new proof must be
generated for the next replace instruction.
Concurrency limit should be determined by empirically testing the demand for
state built on top of SPL Compression.
For instructions on enabling the canopy, see [canopy].

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that controls write-access to the tree
Typically a program, e.g., the Bubblegum contract validates that leaves are valid NFTs.

accounts.name: noop
docs: Program used to emit changelogs as cpi instruction data.

instructions.name: insert_or_append
docs: This instruction takes a proof, and will attempt to write the given leaf
to the specified index in the tree. If the insert operation fails, the leaf will be `append`-ed
to the tree.
It is up to the indexer to parse the final location of the leaf from the emitted changelog.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Authority that controls write-access to the tree
Typically a program, e.g., the Bubblegum contract validates that leaves are valid NFTs.

accounts.name: noop
docs: Program used to emit changelogs as cpi instruction data.

instructions.name: replace_leaf
docs: Note:
Supporting this instruction open a security vulnerability for indexers.
This instruction has been deemed unusable for publicly indexed compressed NFTs.
Indexing batched data in this way requires indexers to read in the `uri`s onto physical storage
and then into their database. This opens up a DOS attack vector, whereby this instruction is
repeatedly invoked, causing indexers to fail.
Because this instruction was deemed insecure, this instruction has been removed
until secure usage is available on-chain.
Executes an instruction that overwrites a leaf node.
Composing programs should check that the data hashed into previous_leaf
matches the authority information necessary to execute this instruction.

instructions.name: transfer_authority
docs: Transfers `authority`.
Requires `authority` to sign

instructions.name: verify_leaf
docs: Verifies a provided proof and leaf.
If invalid, throws an error.

errors.name: IncorrectLeafLength
errors.msg: Incorrect leaf length. Expected vec of 32 bytes

errors.name: ZeroCopyError
errors.msg: Issue zero copying concurrent merkle tree data

errors.name: ConcurrentMerkleTreeConstantsError
errors.msg: An unsupported max depth or max buffer size constant was provided

errors.name: CanopyLengthMismatch
errors.msg: Expected a different byte length for the merkle tree canopy

errors.name: IncorrectAuthority
errors.msg: Provided authority does not match expected tree authority

errors.name: IncorrectAccountOwner
errors.msg: Account is owned by a different program, expected it to be owned by this program

errors.name: IncorrectAccountType
errors.msg: Account provided has incorrect account type

errors.name: LeafIndexOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Leaf index of concurrent merkle tree is out of bounds
